source.title: A Dedicated Population for Reward Coding in the Hippocampus

confidence_rationale: Strong, mechanistically clear evidence for a dedicated CA1 population that encodes reward across environments where standard place cells remap randomly. Directly supports the generalization that hippocampus reserves channel-specific cells for at least one affect stream (reward/goal) beyond pure space.
failure_signatures: If REE were to assume only a single homogeneous map-with-affect-overlay, this paper falsifies it: a dedicated, channel-specific population coexists with the random-remapping ensemble.

Reward-cell activity does not collapse onto sensory cues or stereotyped reward-anticipation behaviour, so the channel cannot be reduced to a sensory- or motor-gated subset of conventional place cells.

mapping.source_claim_statement: A small, specialized CA1/subiculum cell population is consistently active near rewarded goals across environments where most cells remap randomly, suggesting a dedicated channel for reward.
mapping.ree_translation: Reward/goal is one privileged affect-stream that the hippocampal map represents through a dedicated subpopulation rather than via conventional place-cell remapping. This is the strongest candidate for an SD-011-style stream-specific architectural slot beyond harm.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Mouse virtual reality with shifting reward contingencies; not a generic affect channel test. The dedicated-population pattern may be reward-specific (driven by VTA/LC inputs and behavioural relevance) rather than a template for arbitrary affect streams.
mapping.source_context: Mouse, head-fixed virtual-reality linear track, two-photon Ca2+ imaging in CA1 and subiculum, shifting reward contingencies.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron paper, large-scale imaging, internally controlled across multiple environments. Mapping fidelity moderated by the fact that the REE generalization asks about *N* affect streams, while this paper convincingly addresses only one (reward). Transfer risk is bounded — rodent CA1 results carry to the architectural question well, but the bound on whether other affect channels follow the same dedicated-population template is the open problem this lit-pull is scoping.
